question_text: Which statement about gas pressure is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Pressure in a gas comes from particles sinking to the bottom of the container

options.label: B
options.text: Pressure acts equally in all directions because particles collide randomly with walls from all angles

options.label: C
options.text: Pressure only acts downward due to gravity

options.label: D
options.text: Pressure increases when particles move more slowly

correct_answer: Pressure acts equally in all directions because particles collide randomly with walls from all angles
explanation: Gas particles move randomly in all directions, so they collide with container walls equally from every side. This creates uniform pressure in all directions, unlike liquids where pressure increases with depth.
